points.speaker: Annette Lin
points.text: Finalized subcommittee memberships: Mental Health—Nirvana Nwokidu, Jill Rakestraw, and Vice Chair IdaRose Sylvester; Bystander Intervention Training—Kevin Ball, Jill Rakestraw, and Xavier Webb; Housing and Security Storytelling—IdaRose Sylvester, Annette Lin, and Xavier Webb. Julie Solomon will close out prior housing project tasks.
points.source: Laurel James : Mental Health Subcommittee, the interest is in Commissioners Noakito and Rick Straw and Vice Chair Sylvester, who will all be be continuing / Bystander Intervention Training has interest from Commissioners Ball, Rick Straw, and Webb / perhaps it makes sense, Chair Lynn, for you to serve on the Housing and Security Subcommittee until such a time as your term ends

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: Departing commissioners will help launch subcommittee work while seats transition, with memberships revisited in January when new appointees are seated. If overall HRC membership declines, project scopes or workloads may be adjusted to balance capacity.
points.source: Laurel James : typically in January when new commissioners are sworn in and seated, there is a revisiting of the subcommittee appointments / If there is a reduction in the number of total HRC members serving, then it would also be an opportunity to rebalance the workload or to revisit the scope

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: A quorum of four is needed to hold HRC meetings (assuming seven seats). Laurel is seeking permission to share application counts with the Commission. If applicant numbers are low, recruitment can occur year-round.
points.source: IdaRose Sylvester : I believe it's four / Laurel James : I have asked the City Clerk's office if I can have the number of applications and if I'm able to share it / Jill Rakestraw : there is an opportunity to go out and recruit, you know, people to apply at any point during the year

points.speaker: Julie Solomon
points.text: If any of the four wish to record stories for public archiving, Julie will conduct interviews and explore transcription support through Michael Kahn's Stanford research assistant, Camila, who continues working on the project.
points.source: Julie Solomon : if they really are interested to share their story and have it archived and made available to the public / Camilla a research assistant to Michael Kahn from Stanford. Camilla continues to work for Michael / I would be willing to do interviews

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: City staff will enhance the project webpage by adding a video gallery, publishing oral histories for public access, and posting event information and Q&A. Laurel will consult Communications about hosting the video on the City's YouTube channel.
points.source: Laurel James : updates to the web page including adding a video gallery, eventually adding the oral histories and or videos that are available and meant to be shared more publicly / That is a question for our communications team and I will post it to them

points.speaker: Annette Lin
points.text: The agenda was reordered to hear the Multicultural Engagement Program informational update earlier in the meeting so staff could depart sooner; no action was taken on the item.
points.source: Annette Lin : we're moving Section 6.1 new business up on for the Multicultural Engagement Program informational update / No action will be taken on this item

rejected_items.explanation: Similar to review_007, this was discussion of interest and assignments, not a formal decision. The transcript shows 'interest from' language, not commitment or decision language. No formal decision-making process evident.
